verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically a cytosolic protein in healthy cells that, upon apoptotic stimulation, translocates to and inserts into the outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM) to form pores causing MOMP. Its entire biology is confined to the cytosol and mitochondrial outer membrane — not the plasma membrane outer leaflet. No TM domain anchors it to the plasma membrane; no GPI anchor or outer-leaflet lipidation exists; it has no CD designation; there is no documented stable plasma membrane surface pool (no surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, or surface proteomics data placing it on the extracellular face of the PM). The 'outer leaflet' biology is solely at the OMM during apoptosis, not the plasma membrane. No therapeutic programs target BAX on the cell surface (BAX-activating BH3 mimetics like ABT-737 target the intracellular cytosolic/mitochondrial pool). pMHC is not a factor here. All pre-no checklist probes are negative: no cancer ecto-display literature, no ICD-related PM translocation, no sheddase biology, no TM-partner stable surface attachment.
